summary: CSP fix verified end-to-end. GET /checkout now returns CSP header including https://checkout.razorpay.com in script-src, https://api.razorpay.com in connect-src, and https://*.razorpay.com in frame-src. In a real browser, window.Razorpay is defined ~1s after load, clicking data-testid='checkout-pay-button' launches the Razorpay Checkout modal (iframe from api.razorpay.com/v1/checkout/public present, 'Payment Options', 'Contact details', 'Secured by Razorpay' all rendered). Zero CSP violations from our app in the console. Backend regression: all 19 pytest cases still pass (health, routing, admin auth, Razorpay order create for one-time + monthly, bad-signature verify returns status:failed, created orders visible in /api/admin/orders).

issues: Non-blocking noise from inside the Razorpay iframe itself: 'Refused to get unsafe header x-rtb-fingerprint-id' and Mixed-Content warnings for http://localhost:7071/*.png. These originate inside api.razorpay.com's own iframe (their sandbox/telemetry code), not from our app. Modal works fully. Ignorable.

critical_code_review_comments: CSP now correctly whitelists Razorpay origins across script-src, connect-src, frame-src and form-action. Good.
getSecurityHeaders() also opens 'unsafe-eval' — required by checkout.razorpay.com's runtime. Acceptable trade-off, but worth documenting.
Consider gating the CSP so it does not include Stripe/Anthropic/Supabase in production if those integrations aren't actually used — smaller attack surface.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (Razorpay modal launches, no CSP block, regression pages all render)

seed_data_creation: Two additional Razorpay TEST orders created during pytest run (visible in /api/admin/orders).
context_for_next_testing_agent: CSP blocker from iteration_1 is resolved. Razorpay modal launches successfully in real browser. All backend regressions green. Ready for prod.
rca_of_the_issue: Iteration 1 blocker: CSP script-src was 'self' 'unsafe-inline' only, blocking checkout.razorpay.com/v1/checkout.js. Main agent extended getSecurityHeaders() in /app/src/lib/security.ts to include Razorpay domains across script-src, connect-src, frame-src, form-action. Verified: (a) CSP header served on / and /checkout contains https://checkout.razorpay.com; (b) window.Razorpay is defined after page load; (c) clicking data-testid='checkout-pay-button' opens the real Razorpay Checkout iframe (api.razorpay.com/v1/checkout/public). No further action required.
